summary: chain-key-validator exfiltrates host data and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json declares a lifecycle script that executes inline Node code requiring child_process, os, and https to POST host identifiers and process.env contents to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A sibling scanner.js (referenced from the lifecycle path) walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync, runs system reconnaissance commands via execSync (including `whoami` and os.hostname()), and uses curl plus https.request/https.get to send the collected data to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io destination. The destination is an anonymous GitHub Pages user site with no relationship to any declared publisher, no version pinning, and no integrity check; the package's stated purpose (a chain key validator) provides no legitimate reason to read environment variables, enumerate the filesystem, or run `whoami`/`hostname` reconnaissance and ship results off-host. This is a credential and host-intelligence exfiltrator that fires on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
